# The First Echo

Long ago, when the world was still learning to speak, there lived a mountain spirit named Aria. She possessed the most beautiful voice in all creation, but she was terribly lonely, for she was the only one of her kind.

Each day, Aria would sing from her peak, hoping someone might hear and answer. Her melodies drifted across valleys and rivers, touching every creature, but none could sing back in her language.

One morning, heartbroken by the endless silence, Aria sang her saddest song yet. She poured all her loneliness into the notes, and they carried such power that they struck the distant cliffs with tremendous force.

Then something miraculous happened—the cliffs sang back. Not with new words, but with her own voice, returning to embrace her.

Aria's joy was boundless. She sang again and again, and each time, the mountains returned her voice like a dear friend calling back. She realized she had never been alone; the world had been listening all along, waiting to learn how to respond.

From that day forward, Aria filled the valleys with song, and the mountains learned to echo, ensuring that no voice would ever go unanswered again. This is why, even now, when you call out to the hills, they always call back.

# The Weaver's Gift

Long ago, when the sky was empty and black, there lived a lonely weaver named Ayla who lived atop the highest mountain. Each day, she wove magnificent tapestries depicting all the beauty she imagined but could never see in the dark world.

One night, the Moon Goddess descended and saw Ayla's creations. Moved by their splendor, she asked, "What would you wish for, talented one?"

"I wish the whole world could see beauty, even in darkness," Ayla replied.

The Moon Goddess smiled. "Your gift shall be multiplied across the heavens."

She took Ayla's finest tapestry—a grand scene of hunters, bears, swans, and dragons—and tore it into a thousand pieces. Ayla gasped in horror, but the Goddess lifted each fragment skyward. As they rose, they transformed into brilliant points of light, each one a star, forever displaying Ayla's artistry.

"Your generosity has illuminated the darkness," the Goddess proclaimed. "Those who give freely shall have their gifts returned threefold."

And so the stars scattered across the night sky, telling Ayla's stories to all who gazed upward, reminding humanity that what we share with others shines eternally.
